description: Production Redis observability for chrono-queue: throughput, network I/O, CPU utilization, slowlog, cache hit rate, Lettuce client latency and command mix, connection pool health, per-instance breakdown bar gauges, Top-N rankings, and alert-reference SLO guardrails. Variables support multi-instance and multi-application analysis at scale.

panels.description: Command throughput per Redis instance over the selected time window. Per-instance lines surface load imbalance across the fleet. Use the Instance and Application variables to isolate specific targets.
panels.title: Redis Commands Rate

panels.description: Network bytes received by Redis per second, per instance. Correlates with SET/HSET/LPUSH payload sizes used for job ingestion and deduplication. Spikes without a corresponding commands-rate increase indicate larger payloads per command.
panels.title: Redis Network Input Rate

panels.description: Network bytes sent by Redis per second, per instance. Correlates with GET/HGET/LRANGE response sizes from job-worker and retry-dispatcher read traffic. Output > input ratio signals read-heavy workloads from consumers.
panels.title: Redis Network Output Rate

panels.description: Maximum slowlog depth across the selected Redis instances. Alert reference PromQL: max by (instance, service) (redis_slowlog_length{instance=~"$instance"}) > 0
panels.title: Redis Slow Log Depth

panels.description: Top 5 Redis instances by current slowlog depth. Non-zero depth indicates slow commands are accumulating on that instance and should be correlated with CPU and command-mix panels.
panels.title: Top Slow Redis Instances

panels.description: Correlates Redis command throughput with raw process CPU consumption. Rising CPU-seconds/s without proportional command growth indicates increasingly expensive command execution or background work.
panels.title: Commands vs CPU

panels.description: Worst-case p99 Lettuce completion latency across the selected application, instance, and command dimensions. Threshold lines highlight anomaly conditions: warn at 50 ms and critical at 100 ms.
panels.title: Latency Spike Detector

targets.expr: max(histogram_quantile(0.99, sum by (le, application, instance, command) (chrono:lettuce_command_completion_seconds_bucket:rate5m{application=~"${application:regex}", instance=~"$instance", command=~"${command:regex}"})))

panels.description: Core-normalized Redis CPU utilization per instance over time. Warn threshold at 50 % (yellow); critical at 80 % (red). Both core-normalized and total CPU series shown for host-context correlation.
panels.title: Redis CPU Utilization (Core Normalized)

panels.description: Aggregate rejected connection rate across the selected Redis instances. Alert reference PromQL: sum by (instance, service) (increase(redis_rejected_connections_total{instance=~"$instance"}[$__rate_interval])) > 0
panels.title: Redis Rejected Connections Rate

panels.description: Redis command throughput by instance and application label. This keeps breakdowns stable when the dashboard later scales to multiple Redis targets.
panels.title: Redis Commands Processed Rate

panels.description: Bytes received from all clients per second. Correlates with payload sizes of SET/HSET/LPUSH commands used for job deduplication.
panels.title: Redis Network Input Rate

panels.description: Bytes sent to all clients per second. Correlates with GET/HGET/LRANGE responses from Redis.
panels.title: Redis Network Output Rate

panels.description: CPU time-series with SRE SLO threshold lines: warn at 70 % (orange) and critical at 85 % (red) per core. Alert PromQL: 100 * sum by (instance, service) (rate(redis_cpu_sys_seconds_total{instance=~"$instance"}[$__rate_interval]) + rate(redis_cpu_user_seconds_total{instance=~"$instance"}[$__rate_interval])) > 80. Both total and normalized CPU series shown for correlation with host-level context.
panels.title: Redis CPU Utilization Over Time

panels.description: Lettuce avg and max command latency split by application, instance, and command. SLO threshold lines: warn at 10 ms (0.010 s, yellow), critical at 50 ms (0.050 s, red). Use the Application and Command variables to isolate noisy callers before attributing latency to Redis itself.
panels.title: Lettuce Command Latency (Avg and Max)

targets.expr: max by (application, instance, command) (lettuce_command_completion_seconds_max{application=~"${application:regex}", instance=~"$instance", command=~"${command:regex}"})

panels.description: Histogram-based Redis client latency percentiles (p50 / p95 / p99) from Lettuce command completion buckets. SLO threshold lines: warn at 50 ms (0.050 s, yellow), critical at 100 ms (0.100 s, red). Latency spike on p99 without p50 movement indicates tail-latency regression rather than global slowdown. Alert PromQL (p95): histogram_quantile(0.95, ...) > 0.100.
panels.title: Lettuce Command Completion Latency Percentiles

targets.expr: histogram_quantile(0.50, sum by (le, application, instance, command) (chrono:lettuce_command_completion_seconds_bucket:rate5m{application=~"${application:regex}", instance=~"$instance", command=~"${command:regex}"}))

histogram_quantile(0.95, sum by (le, application, instance, command) (chrono:lettuce_command_completion_seconds_bucket:rate5m{application=~"${application:regex}", instance=~"$instance", command=~"${command:regex}"}))

histogram_quantile(0.99, sum by (le, application, instance, command) (chrono:lettuce_command_completion_seconds_bucket:rate5m{application=~"${application:regex}", instance=~"$instance", command=~"${command:regex}"}))

panels.description: Lettuce command throughput by application, instance, and command. This is the primary workload-shaping panel for correlating specific command mix changes with Redis saturation.
panels.title: Lettuce Command Throughput

panels.description: Firing instance count. Alert PromQL: min by (instance, service) (redis_up{instance=~"$instance"}) == 0
panels.title: Alert: Redis Down Instances

targets.expr: sum((min by (instance, service) (redis_up{instance=~"$instance"}) == bool 0) or vector(0))

panels.description: Firing instance count. Alert PromQL: sum by (instance, service) (increase(redis_rejected_connections_total{instance=~"$instance"}[$__rate_interval])) > 0
panels.title: Alert: Rejected Connections

panels.description: Firing instance count. Alert PromQL: sum by (instance, service) (increase(redis_evicted_keys_total{instance=~"$instance"}[$__rate_interval])) > 0
panels.title: Alert: Key Evictions

panels.description: Firing instance count. Alert PromQL: 100 * sum by (instance, service) (rate(redis_cpu_sys_seconds_total{instance=~"$instance"}[$__rate_interval]) + rate(redis_cpu_user_seconds_total{instance=~"$instance"}[$__rate_interval])) > 80
panels.title: Alert: CPU Above 80%

panels.description: Firing instance count. Alert PromQL: max by (instance, service) (redis_slowlog_length{instance=~"$instance"}) > 0
panels.title: Alert: Slowlog Activity

targets.expr: sum((max by (instance, service) (redis_slowlog_length{instance=~"$instance"}) > bool 0) or vector(0))

panels.description: Active and pending Redis client connections per application/instance. Sustained pending growth indicates pool saturation or downstream Redis latency. Full-width time-series replaces per-instance stat panels for richer trend context.
panels.title: Redis Pool Connections Over Time

targets.expr: (max by (application, instance) (lettuce_connections_active{application=~"${application:regex}", instance=~"$instance"}) or max by (application, instance) (lettuce_pool_allocated{application=~"${application:regex}", instance=~"$instance"}))

(max by (application, instance) (lettuce_pool_pending{application=~"${application:regex}", instance=~"$instance"}) or max by (application, instance) (lettuce_connections_pending{application=~"${application:regex}", instance=~"$instance"}))

panels.description: Key count growth rate per second by instance/application, derived from keyspace gauge slope over a 1h window. Sustained positive growth indicates rising cardinality and memory pressure risk.
panels.title: Redis Key Growth Rate

panels.description: Used-memory growth rate in bytes/s by instance/application, derived from a 1h slope. Use this to forecast runway before maxmemory or host limits are reached.
panels.title: Redis Memory Growth Rate

panels.description: Command throughput growth versus one hour ago by instance/application. Positive values indicate workload expansion; negative values indicate contraction.
panels.title: Redis Command Growth Trend (1h)

panels.description: Correlates Redis command throughput against p95 client latency by instance and application. Right-axis threshold lines: warn at 50 ms (yellow), critical at 100 ms (red) — matching the p95 latency SLO. A latency spike that tracks command rate growth is load-driven; divergence typically indicates a Redis-side regression.
panels.title: Correlation: Commands vs p95 Latency

targets.expr: 1000 * histogram_quantile(0.95, sum by (le, application, instance, command) (chrono:lettuce_command_completion_seconds_bucket:rate5m{application=~"${application:regex}", instance=~"$instance", command=~"${command:regex}"}))

panels.description: Correlates active client connections against rejected connection rate per Redis instance. Right-axis threshold line at > 0 (red): any rejected connection fires the alert. Alert PromQL: sum by (instance, service) (increase(redis_rejected_connections_total{instance=~"$instance"}[$__rate_interval])) > 0. Rejected connection spikes near the connection ceiling indicate maxclients pressure or upstream pool exhaustion.
panels.title: Correlation: Connections vs Rejected Connections

panels.description: Correlates Redis CPU utilization (sys+user, % of one core) against command throughput by instance and application. A rising CPU-per-command ratio flags increasingly expensive workloads. Threshold lines at 70 % and 85 % match SLO alert levels.
panels.title: Correlation: CPU Utilization vs Command Rate

panels.description: Correlates Redis used-memory growth against eviction rate by instance and service. Right-axis threshold line at > 0 (red): any eviction fires the alert. Alert PromQL: sum by (instance, service) (increase(redis_evicted_keys_total{instance=~"$instance"}[$__rate_interval])) > 0. Rising memory with concurrent non-zero evictions confirms hard memory pressure and potential data churn — the primary signal for maxmemory tuning or capacity expansion.
panels.title: Correlation: Memory Used vs Eviction Rate

panels.description: Cache hit rate by Redis instance/application label. Falling hit rate can mean a working-set shift, key churn, or TTL/eviction behaviour mismatch.
panels.title: Cache Hit Rate Over Time

panels.description: Current memory usage per Redis instance, Top-10 ranked. Surfaces the most memory-pressured instance instantly without scanning timeseries legends.
panels.title: Memory Used by Instance (Current)

panels.description: Current CPU utilisation per Redis instance, Top-10 ranked. Identifies hot instances in multi-instance deployments at a glance. Threshold lines at 50 % (warn) and 80 % (critical) align with SLO alerts.
panels.title: CPU % by Instance (Current)

panels.description: Current command throughput per Redis instance, Top-10 ranked. Reveals load imbalance across a multi-instance fleet and correlates with the CPU utilisation bar gauge to the left.
panels.title: Command Rate by Instance (Current)

panels.description: Top-10 Redis commands ranked by p99 Lettuce completion latency (ms). Instantly identifies the worst tail-latency offenders for targeted optimization. Use the Command variable to drill down.
panels.title: Top-10 Highest Latency Commands (p99)

targets.expr: topk(10, 1000 * histogram_quantile(0.99, sum by (le, application, instance, command) (chrono:lettuce_command_completion_seconds_bucket:rate5m{application=~"${application:regex}", instance=~"$instance", command=~"${command:regex}"})))

panels.description: Top-10 Redis instances by current command throughput. Surfaces which instance handles the most traffic in a multi-instance fleet — the primary input for load balancing decisions.
panels.title: Top-10 Most Active Instances (cmd/s)

panels.description: Top-10 Redis instances by current memory usage. Surfaces instances closest to maxmemory limit for proactive capacity planning before evictions begin.
panels.title: Top-10 Highest Memory Usage Instances

panels.description: Top-10 Redis instances ranked by p99 Lettuce client completion latency (ms). Computes quantile from the recording-rule bucket series, collapsing application and command dimensions so the ranking is purely by instance. Drill into 'Lettuce Command Completion Latency Percentiles' to see the per-command breakdown for the identified hot instance.
panels.title: Top-10 Highest Latency Instances (p99)

targets.expr: topk(10, 1000 * histogram_quantile(0.99, sum by (le, application, instance, command) (chrono:lettuce_command_completion_seconds_bucket:rate5m{instance=~"$instance"})))

panels.description: Top-10 Redis instances ranked by current rejected connection rate (connections/s). Instances with non-zero rejected connections are at or over maxclients capacity. Use alongside 'Correlation: Connections vs Rejected Connections' to confirm whether the ceiling is being hit under load.
panels.title: Top-10 Highest Rejected Connection Rate

panels.description: Top-10 Redis instances by current slowlog queue depth. Any non-zero depth indicates slow commands that exceeded slowlog-log-slower-than. Growing depth under sustained load typically indicates KEYS/SCAN over large keyspaces, blocking commands, or tight CPU. Correlate with 'CPU Utilization Over Time' and 'Lettuce Command Completion Latency Percentiles' on the same instance.
panels.title: Top-10 Highest Slowlog Depth Instances

panels.description: Count of application × instance combinations where p95 Lettuce latency exceeds the 100 ms SLO threshold. Alert PromQL: histogram_quantile(0.95, sum by (le, application, instance, command) (chrono:lettuce_command_completion_seconds_bucket:rate5m{instance=~"$instance", application=~"$application"})) > 0.100
panels.title: Alert: p95 Latency > 100 ms

targets.expr: sum(  histogram_quantile(0.95, sum by (le, application, instance) (    chrono:lettuce_command_completion_seconds_bucket:rate5m{instance=~"$instance"}  )) > bool 0.100 or vector(0))

panels.description: Count of application × instance combinations where p99 Lettuce latency exceeds the 250 ms critical SLO threshold. Alert PromQL: histogram_quantile(0.99, sum by (le, application, instance, command) (chrono:lettuce_command_completion_seconds_bucket:rate5m{instance=~"$instance", application=~"$application"})) > 0.250
panels.title: Alert: p99 Latency > 250 ms

targets.expr: sum(  histogram_quantile(0.99, sum by (le, application, instance) (    chrono:lettuce_command_completion_seconds_bucket:rate5m{instance=~"$instance"}  )) > bool 0.250 or vector(0))

title: Redis Performance and Latency
